title: Installation
content:
Git Installation on Windows#This guide walks you through installing Git on a Windows machine.
Download Git#Visit the official Git website: https://git-scm.com Click the Download for Windows button The installer will automatically detect your system (32-bit or 64-bit) and download the appropriate version Run the Installer#Open the downloaded .exe file Click Next to proceed through the installation wizard Installation Options#Step 1: Select Components#Keep the default selections checked: ✓ Git Bash Here ✓ Git GUI Here ✓ Git LFS (Large File Support) ✓ Associate .git* configuration files with the default text editor Step 2: Choose Default Editor#Select your preferred text editor (Notepad++, VS Code, or Vim) If unsure, choose Nano as it’s beginner-friendly Step 3: Configure PATH Environment#Select Git from the command line and also from 3rd-party software This allows you to use Git in any terminal application Step 4: HTTPS Transport Backend#Select Use the native Windows Secure Channel library This uses Windows’ built-in SSL/TLS for secure connections Step 5: Line Ending Conversions#Select Checkout as-is, commit as-is (recommended for cross-platform projects) Or select Checkout Windows-style, commit Unix-style if you’re working on Windows only Step 6: Terminal Emulator#Select Use Windows’ default console window MinTTY is also a good option if you prefer a Unix-like terminal Step 7: Extra Options#Check Enable file system caching for better performance Check Enable Git Credential Manager for easier authentication Complete Installation#Click Install to start the installation Wait for the process to complete (usually takes 1-2 minutes) Click Finish Verify Installation#Open Command Prompt or PowerShell and run:
git --versionYou should see output like: git version 2.43.0.windows.1
Initial Configuration#Configure your Git identity for commits:
git config --global user.name "Your Name" git config --global user.email "your.email@example.com"Verify the configuration:
git config --global --listNext Steps#Learn the basic Git commands Set up a GitHub account if you haven’t already Clone your first repository and start version control!

title: About Me
content:
About Me#Hello! I’m Nadec Biju, a Mechanical Engineering student at CUSAT (2022-2026) passionate about digital fabrication, robotics, and innovative product development.
Contact#📞 7558804529 | 📍 Kochi, Kerala | 📧 nadecbiju6@gmail.com | 💼 LinkedIn
Skills#CAD & Design: Solidworks, Fusion 360 | Fabrication: 3D Printing, Laser Cutting, CNC | Programming: Python, ESP32, OpenCV
My Journey#Apr 2025 - PresentTeam Captain — Tarusa MotorsportsLeading 30+ member team designing Hydrogen Baja vehicles. Overseeing CAD, vehicle dynamics, and manufacturing.
Aug 2025 - PresentTrackside Intern — Indian Motorsports AcademyWorking with professional race engineers in Formula 4 and Indian Racing League championships.
Jan 2025 - PresentFounder — Layer6Founded CAD design and 3D printing consultancy for prototyping and product design services.
Jan 2025 - Aug 2025Product Development Intern — Pre6 TechFounding team intern at AI startup by Amod Malviya (Founder of Udaan, ex-CTO Flipkart).
Apr 2024 - Mar 2025Team Manager — Tarusa MotorsportsLed team to AIR 7 Overall, AIR 1 Cost Award, AIR 1 Statics, AIR 2 Simulation.
Mar 2024 - Apr 2025Vice Chairman — SAE CUSATLed student committee, managed flagship events: Inventiva, SAE INDIA Tier Events, and Cadable.
Sep 2023 - Feb 2024Design Lead — Hermes CUSATLed design for SAE Bicycle Challenge. Secured AIR 5 and 1st Place Best Design.
Dec 2023 - Feb 2025Resident Maker & Intern — TinkerHubManaged 3D printers, mentored students on hardware projects, conducted maker events.
Nov 2023 - Nov 2025Intern — CUSAT FablabDeveloped solid-state fan using piezoelectric crystals. Applied rapid prototyping techniques.
Jan 2023 - Mar 2024Powertrain Member — Tarusa MotorsportsWorked on powertrain for Hydrogen-BAJA. Team achieved AIR 1.
Certifications#🏆 SOLIDWORKS Additive Manufacturing Associate – Dassault Systèmes 🏆 SOLIDWORKS CAD Design Associate – Dassault Systèmes 🏆 SOLIDWORKS Electrical Design Associate – Dassault Systèmes 🏆 SOLIDWORKS Sustainability Associate – Dassault Systèmes FAB Academy is my opportunity to further develop skills in digital fabrication, electronics, and rapid prototyping—making advanced manufacturing accessible and innovative.

title: Agreement
content:
Fab Academy Student Agreement#The Fab Academy is responsible for:#Teaching principles and practices of digital fabrication Arranging lectures, recitations, meetings, and events for the class Evaluating and providing feedback on student work Offering clear standards for completing assignments Certifying and archiving student progress Supervising class preparation Reviewing prospective students, instructors, and labs Providing central staff and infrastructure for students, instructors, and labs Fund-raising for costs not covered by student tuition Managing and reporting on the program’s finances, results, and impacts Publicizing the program Promoting a respectful environment free of harassment and discrimination Encouraging a diverse, accessible, and equitable community I am a Fab Academy student, responsible for:#Attending class lectures and participating in reviews Developing and documenting projects assigned to introduce and demonstrate skills, in enough detail to be able to reproduce them Honestly reporting on my work, and appropriately attributing the work of others (both human and machine, including AI prompts) Being prepared to answer questions about my work Allowing the Fab Academy to share my work (with attribution) in the class for purposes compatible with its mission Working safely Leaving workspaces in the same (or better) condition than I found them Participating in the upkeep of my lab Ensuring that my tuition for local and central class costs is covered Following locally applicable health and safety guidance Recognizing limits on lab and staff time Promoting a respectful environment free of harassment and discrimination Signed by committing this file in my repository:
Nadec Biju
View Student Agreement Document

title: Git Installation
content:
Installing Git#Git is a distributed version control system essential for collaborative development and tracking project changes.
Windows#Download the installer from git-scm.com Run the installer and follow the setup wizard Choose default options or customize based on your preferences Open Command Prompt or PowerShell and verify: git --version macOS#Using Homebrew: brew install git Verify installation: git --version Linux#Using package manager: # Ubuntu/Debian sudo apt-get install git # Fedora sudo dnf install git Verify installation: git --version Initial Configuration#After installation, configure Git with your details:
git config --global user.name "Your Name" git config --global user.email "your.email@example.com"Verify your configuration:
git config --list
